goal: Instrument a 3-station bin-picking cell for per-station cycle time, emit CSV, render a matplotlib Gantt — acknowledging Isaac Sim has no built-in VC-style cycle time chart.
thoughts: Honest up-front: no VC-style Gantt chart ships with Isaac Sim 5.x. Replacement: timestamp each waypoint/station transition from the motion script, write to CSV, render in matplotlib or Plotly. Must break out robot move time (pick/transfer/place/retract), conveyor dwell, and fixture clamp — not just total. Isaac Sim is continuous-physics-first; discrete-event sim tools (VC, Plant Sim) win here natively. Do not oversell — if cycle-time alone is Kenji's job, stay in VC.
code:
# 1. Anchor the honest scope: no built-in Gantt
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim cycle time event logging Gantt chart discrete event simulation gap")

# 2. Inject waypoint-transition timestamp logger (tiny, runs alongside the motion script)
run_usd_script(script="""
import time, csv, os
STATIONS = ['infeed_conveyor','robot_pick','robot_transfer','robot_place','fixture_clamp','outfeed']
LOG_PATH = '<CSV_OUT>'
def log_event(station, event):
    new = not os.path.exists(LOG_PATH)
    with open(LOG_PATH, 'a', newline='') as f:
        w = csv.writer(f)
        if new: w.writerow(['t','station','event'])
        w.writerow([time.perf_counter(), station, event])

# Wire log_event(station, 'enter') / log_event(station, 'exit') calls around each waypoint in
# the motion script -- no OmniGraph needed. CSV schema: t, station, event.
print('logger ready -- instrument motion script at each station boundary')
""")

# 3. Post-process: CSV -> Gantt in matplotlib (50 lines)
# import pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
# df = pd.read_csv('<CSV_OUT>')
# spans = df.pivot_table(index='station', columns='event', values='t').reset_index()
# spans['dur'] = spans['exit'] - spans['enter']
# plt.barh(spans['station'], spans['dur'], left=spans['enter'])
# plt.xlabel('t (s)'); plt.title('Cycle Gantt'); plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig('<PNG_OUT>')

# 4. Bundle the cell + logger for reproducible runs
export_scene_package(source_stage="<STAGE>", output_path="<BUNDLE>")
failure_modes: Claiming a built-in Isaac Sim Gantt chart exists — Kenji will not trust anything after
Logging only total cycle time — can't see which station is the bottleneck
Using time.time() instead of time.perf_counter() — wall-clock jumps under NTP slew
Oversell Isaac Sim for cycle-time-only jobs — arguably VC or Plant Sim wins here
